meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve no version control (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve No Version Control
content.seo_description: Learn how to implement user onboarding to mitigate issues from lack of version control. A beginner guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for students.
content.answer_block: Without version control, a single mistake destroys your progress. By implementing robust user onboarding, you create a controlled entry point that logs user states and captures session data. This architecture allows you to track application health and recover user context, effectively creating an audit trail that acts as a safety net.
content.intro:
You are staring at a screen of code that worked ten minutes ago, but now refuses to compile. You didn't push to a repository, and there is no 'undo' button for the hours of logic you just refactored. The panic is real, but the lesson is vital: building without version control is a rite of passage, yet it is a trap that kills developer momentum.

Today, we pivot. Instead of relying on manual file saves, we will build a user onboarding flow that forces state management and session tracking. By integrating Antigravity and Claude Code, you will not only secure your user's journey but also stabilize your development environment with automated, high-fidelity tracking.

steps.title: Define the Onboarding Schema
steps.description: Establish a rigid data structure for your user onboarding to ensure every session state is captured, preventing data loss during crashes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a JSON schema for a user onboarding flow that captures step-by-step progress, including timestamp, state_id, and input_data.

steps.title: Implement Session Persistence
steps.description: Hook your onboarding flow into a local persistence layer so that if your app crashes, the user's progress is saved.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a persistence layer using localStorage that saves the current onboarding step and user input on every change.

steps.title: Build the Progress Recovery Logic
steps.description: Create a check function that runs on app load to detect existing sessions, effectively simulating a rollback feature.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a recovery function that checks for existing onboarding sessions and redirects the user to the last incomplete step.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement recovery check in app.js'

steps.title: Integrate Error Boundary Logging
steps.description: Wrap your onboarding components in error boundaries that log the user's current state before a crash occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a React Error Boundary component that captures the current application state and logs it to the console.

steps.title: Automate State Documentation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to document your state transitions so you can manually recreate your work if a file is corrupted.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a markdown document detailing the state flow of my onboarding sequence for future reference.

pro_tips: Always keep a 'Snapshot' folder with a timestamped JSON copy of your current state logic; it's the poor man's version control.
Use console.table() to visualize your onboarding state object in real-time during development.
Implement a 'Reset' button that clears local storage; it saves you from getting stuck in broken states during testing.
Treat your onboarding inputs as immutable objects to prevent accidental state corruption during user interactions.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests for your state transitions before writing the UI code to ensure logic integrity.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is stable, your next milestone is to containerize your project with Docker and host it on a platform like Vercel or Railway to professionalize your workflow.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are an architect building resilient systems. Stop fearing the 'undo' button and start building with confidence. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
